title: Product: Roadmap Planning
summary: Matt Watson, Vinnie Micciche, Michael Donovan, and Thomas Howe discussed improving portal user engagement, citing challenges with onboarding, communication, and training. Key suggestions included focusing on the portal's value proposition, leveraging a product-led growth model and diffusion of innovation theory, implementing pilot programs to test GPT, and improving internal team training through a tiered approach. Next steps involve creating engaging training materials, conducting pilot programs with select dealers, and improving data collection and analysis to measure the impact of implemented changes.

decisions.description: Create engaging training materials for portal value communication.
Conduct pilot programs with select dealers to test and improve GPT.
Improve data collection and analysis to measure user engagement impact.
Implement a tiered 'karate school' approach for internal team training.

actionItems.task: Create engaging training and outreach materials for portal value.
Conduct pilot programs with a small group of dealers to test GPT.
Improve data collection and measurement of user engagement.
Establish baselines and organize existing data for product growth measurement.
Design internal team training on portal usage and value communication.

tensions: Challenges with portal onboarding, communication, and training.
Issues with signups, emails, MFA, and inviting new dealer employees.
Sales and client success teams lack effective portal value communication training.
Team members unable to perform demos and inconsistent feature communication.
Lack of understanding of dealer needs contributes to low engagement.
Uncertainty if low usage is product-related or due to communication/training.

contributions.description: Initiated discussion on improving user engagement with the portal.
Presented a product-led growth model highlighting onboarding, activation, retention challenges.
Proposed addressing low engagement via improved onboarding, marketing, and product value.
Acknowledged the need for improved data collection and measurement of user engagement.
Suggested focusing on the 'why' behind portal usage for dealer adoption.
Pointed out sales/client success teams need improved training on portal value.
Proposed a pilot program with dealers to test and improve GPT.
Stressed importance of internal team training before expanding to dealers.
Suggested leveraging diffusion of innovation theory, focusing on engaged users.
Suggested using gamification to incentivize user participation.
Added that lack of understanding dealer needs contributes to low engagement.
Emphasized establishing baselines and organizing existing data for proper growth measurement.
Suggested a 'karate school' approach for tiered internal team training.
Emphasized making portal usage a habit by integrating it into existing workflows.
Suggested incorporating daily prompts to help managers track tasks and improve usage.
